title: Consultation opens on five joint UK pelagic FMPs
authors: FMP team, Scottish Government's Marine Directorate

content.text: Today, the Scottish Government is launching a public consultation on five proposed joint UK fisheries management plans (FMPs).

The FMPs were developed jointly by Defra, the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA) in Northern Ireland, the Welsh Government and the Scottish Government which coordinated the work.

The plans bring together evidence about the current state of these pelagic fish stocks and how they are managed. They also set out policies and actions to restore stocks where needed and keep them healthy and sustainable for the future.
Read the consultation document and submit your response.
The consultation closes at 11:55pm on 14 October 2026.
